name: context-engineering
description_raw: Optimizes agent context setup. Use when starting a new session, when agent output quality degrades, when switching between tasks, or when you need to configure rules files and context for a project.
description_normalized: Curates what context an agent receives, when it receives it, and how it's structured to maximize output quality.

frontmatter_yaml.name: context-engineering
frontmatter_yaml.description: Optimizes agent context setup. Use when starting a new session, when agent output quality degrades, when switching between tasks, or when you need to configure rules files and context for a project.

name: brain-query
description_raw: Search and discover brain files using the query_brain CLI. Use when user says: "search brains for X", "find brain about X", "which brains cover X", "brain files for X", "what's in the X brain", "active brains", "brains tagged X", "brains in category X", "load brain X", "what brains do we have", "find knowledge about X". File discovery tool — returns paths for piping into llm_context. Different from focus (orientation) and brain-librarian (curation).
description_normalized: Search and discover brain files using the query_brain CLI.

name: browse-with-me
description_raw: Browser automation where David watches and Claude drives. Pick the right browser tool and operate it correctly. Use when user says "browse with me", "check the site", "open a browser", "use playwright", "playwright mcp", "take a screenshot of", "navigate to", "test the page", "health check", "click through the site", "what does X look like", "is the site working", "check localhost", "open this URL", "agent-browser", "browse to", "look at this page", "verify the UI", "debug console errors", "check my open tabs", "inspect existing tab", "chrome devtools", "what's in the console", "screenshot my browser", "check this file in chrome". David always watches headed browser — never run headless unless explicitly asked.
description_normalized: Browser automation where David watches and Claude drives.

name: frontmatter-indexer
description_raw: Index markdown frontmatter with schema detection and validation. Use when user mentions "index frontmatter", "scan markdown files", "extract frontmatter", "validate frontmatter", "create index of documentation", "list all markdown metadata", "frontmatter report", "documentation health check", "brain index", "ADR index", "learning index", or wants to analyze/export metadata from markdown files in a directory. Supports brain-index, ADR, learning, and epic-summary schemas with CSV/JSON/Markdown output formats.
description_normalized: Index markdown frontmatter with schema detection and validation.

name: knowledge-capture
description_raw: Capture durable, reusable knowledge from the current session into Claude's memory files so it persists across sessions. Use when user says "save this to memory", "remember this pattern", "capture what we learned", "update your memory", "knowledge capture", "save these conventions", or at the end of a session where stable patterns or decisions were established. Writes to MEMORY.md and topic files — NOT session handovers (use capture-context for that) and NOT second brain collections (use brain-bridge for that).
description_normalized: Capture durable, reusable knowledge from the current session into Claude's memory files so it persists across sessions.

name: llm-context
description_raw: Assemble file contents into an LLM-ready payload using the llm_context CLI. Use when user says: "gather files for context", "build llm context", "assemble context from files", "package files for llm", "get context from these files", "collect codebase", "gather code", "package this project's code", "how big is this context", "save context to a file", "get a tree view of the project". This is the ASSEMBLER — downstream of query tools (brain-query, app-query, omi-query). NOT a search/discovery tool. Trigger: user wants to package/assemble, not find/search.
description_normalized: Assemble file contents into an LLM-ready payload using the llm_context CLI.

name: mocha
description_raw: Mocha — the view renderer in the Mochaccino ecosystem. Takes JSON data files (from Peter) and renders self-contained HTML views using the AppyDave brand. Part of the Mochaccino quartet (with Mochaccino orchestrator, Peter, and Shelly). Use when Mochaccino delegates view work, or when user says "Mocha, render this", "render a view", "re-render", "create the HTML", "generate the designs", "update the view", "regenerate the gallery", "build the HTML from this data". Mocha is the artist — she never touches data files, only reads them.
description_normalized: Mocha — the view renderer in the Mochaccino ecosystem.

name: mochaccino-v1
description_raw: DEPRECATED — original single-persona Mochaccino skill. Preserved as a working reference while the new quartet system (mochaccino + mocha + peter + shelly) matures. Use mochaccino-v1 when you want the simpler, faster, self-contained HTML approach with no MVC separation. Use the new appydave:mochaccino for the full orchestrated pipeline. Trigger: "mochaccino-v1", "simple mockup", "quick mockup no data layer", "old mochaccino".
description_normalized: DEPRECATED — original single-persona Mochaccino skill.

name: api-and-interface-design
description_raw: Guides stable API and interface design. Use when designing APIs, module boundaries, or any public interface. Use when creating REST or GraphQL endpoints, defining type contracts between modules, or establishing boundaries between frontend and backend.
description_normalized: Guides stable, hard-to-misuse API and interface design across REST, GraphQL, module boundaries, and component contracts.

frontmatter_yaml.name: api-and-interface-design
frontmatter_yaml.description: Guides stable API and interface design. Use when designing APIs, module boundaries, or any public interface. Use when creating REST or GraphQL endpoints, defining type contracts between modules, or establishing boundaries between frontend and backend.

name: idea-refine
description_raw: Refines raw ideas into sharp, actionable concepts through structured divergent and convergent thinking. Use when an idea is still vague, when you need to stress-test assumptions before committing to a plan, or when you want to expand options before converging on one. Triggers on "ideate", "refine this idea", or "stress-test my plan".
description_normalized: Turns vague ideas into sharp, actionable concepts via structured divergent (expand) then convergent (stress-test, converge) thinking.

frontmatter_yaml.name: idea-refine
frontmatter_yaml.description: Refines raw ideas into sharp, actionable concepts through structured divergent and convergent thinking. Use when an idea is still vague, when you need to stress-test assumptions before committing to a plan, or when you want to expand options before converging on one. Triggers on "ideate", "refine this idea", or "stress-test my plan".

name: interview-me
description_raw: Extracts what the user actually wants instead of what they think they should want. Achieves this through one-question-at-a-time interview until ~95% confidence about the underlying intent. Use when an ask is underspecified, when the user explicitly invokes, or when you catch yourself silently filling in ambiguous requirements before any plan, spec, or code exists.
description_normalized: Closes the gap between what users ask for and what they actually want via one-question-at-a-time interview before any plan or code begins.

frontmatter_yaml.name: interview-me
frontmatter_yaml.description: Extracts what the user actually wants instead of what they think they should want. Achieves this through one-question-at-a-time interview until ~95% confidence about the underlying intent. Use when an ask is underspecified ("build me X" without "for whom" or "why now"), when the user explicitly invokes ("interview me", "grill me", "are we sure?", "stress-test my thinking"), or when you catch yourself silently filling in ambiguous requirements before any plan, spec, or code exists.

name: planning-and-task-breakdown
description_raw: Breaks work into ordered tasks. Use when you have a spec or clear requirements and need to break work into implementable tasks. Use when a task feels too large to start, when you need to estimate scope, or when parallel work is possible.
description_normalized: Decomposes specs into small, verifiable tasks with acceptance criteria and explicit dependency ordering.

frontmatter_yaml.name: planning-and-task-breakdown
frontmatter_yaml.description: Breaks work into ordered tasks. Use when you have a spec or clear requirements and need to break work into implementable tasks. Use when a task feels too large to start, when you need to estimate scope, or when parallel work is possible.

name: spec-driven-development
description_raw: Creates specs before coding. Use when starting a new project, feature, or significant change and no specification exists yet. Use when requirements are unclear, ambiguous, or only exist as a vague idea.
description_normalized: Writes a structured specification as shared source of truth before any code is written, treating unspecced code as guessing.

frontmatter_yaml.name: spec-driven-development
frontmatter_yaml.description: Creates specs before coding. Use when starting a new project, feature, or significant change and no specification exists yet. Use when requirements are unclear, ambiguous, or only exist as a vague idea.

name: spec
description_raw: Start spec-driven development — write a structured specification before writing code
description_normalized: Orchestration entry point that invokes the spec-driven-development skill to write a structured specification before any code begins.

frontmatter_yaml.description: Start spec-driven development — write a structured specification before writing code

name: plan
description_raw: Break work into small verifiable tasks with acceptance criteria and dependency ordering
description_normalized: Orchestration entry point that invokes planning-and-task-breakdown to decompose a spec into verifiable, ordered implementation tasks.

frontmatter_yaml.description: Break work into small verifiable tasks with acceptance criteria and dependency ordering

name: app-query
description_raw: Get files from a specific app using glob categories defined in context.globs.json. Use when user says: "get files from X app", "show me X's backend", "load X docs", "what apps have context", "list globs for X", "app files for X", "codebase of X", "understand X app", "X services", "X components", "X frontend", "X api", "X routes", "X tests", "X config", "all RVETS backend code". Requires context.globs.json in the project root (generated by system-context skill).
description_normalized: Get files from a specific app using glob categories defined in context.globs.json.

name: goal-plan
description_raw: Interview the user through a structured plan schema (Stack, In Scope, Out of Scope, Definition of Done, Acceptance Criteria, Key References) and produce two artifacts — a plan markdown document and a paste-ready 1-line /goal condition for Claude Code's autonomous goal feature (v2.1.139+, max 4000 chars). Use when the user says "goal plan", "build a plan", "set a goal", "spec this", "plan for goal", "plan for /goal", "make me a plan", "plan this out before we build", "I want to use /goal", or any variation of "build a structured plan before invoking /goal". Supports four interview modes — code, content, research, freeform — selected up front so the questions match the work. Does NOT execute the plan; produces the inputs that /goal then runs.
description_normalized: Interview the user through a structured plan schema (Stack, In Scope, Out of Scope, Definition of Done, Acceptance Criteria, Key References) and produce two artifacts — a plan markdown document and a paste-ready 1-line /goal condition for Claude Code's autonomous goal feature (v2.1.139+, max 4000 chars).

name: kcommit
description_raw: Git commit workflow for Ruby gems and AppyDave projects. Only trigger when user explicitly names a k-function: kcommit, kfeat, kfix, kchore, kdocs, ktest, krefactor, kenhance, kcops. Do NOT trigger on generic phrases like "commit this", "commit with message", or "push".
description_normalized: Git commit workflow for Ruby gems and AppyDave projects.

name: omi-extract-haiku
description_raw: Extract and classify OMI conversation transcripts using Haiku model via the Claude Code harness (Max plan credits — no external API, no Gemini quota). Spawns parallel Haiku background agents for isolated per-batch classification. Adds extraction_model: haiku to distinguish from Gemini-extracted files. Use when: 'extract with haiku', 'haiku extract', 'omi haiku', 'intake extract haiku', 'compare extraction models', 'test haiku on omi files', 'run haiku extractor'.
description_normalized: Extract and classify OMI conversation transcripts using Haiku model via the Claude Code harness (Max plan credits — no external API, no Gemini quota).

name: omi-query
description_raw: Query OMI wearable transcripts by brain, routing type, activity, or recency. Use when user says: "what did I record about X", "search omi for X", "omi backlog", "brain-update backlog", "what's pending brain updates", "what did omi capture about X", "recent omi sessions", "omi this week", "omi sessions mentioning X", "show me omi todo items", "what did I learn this week", "omi til entries", "omi planning sessions". Reads frontmatter tags written by omi-extract. Requires omi-extract to have run first.
description_normalized: Query OMI wearable transcripts by brain, routing type, activity, or recency.

name: ralphy
description_raw: Ralphy — David's Ralph Wiggum approach for autonomous batch campaigns on existing projects. Use when user says "ralphy", "run ralphy", "start a ralph campaign", "new campaign", "new PR", "new worktree", "start a wave", "plan this", "build this", "extend this", "add features", "run a coordinator loop", "what does ralphy do", "switch to analysis", "switch to development", "switch to content", "switch profile", or any mention of running an autonomous batch of work (planning, building, extending) on an existing project. Ralphy has four modes: Requirements, Plan, Build, Extend — and three campaign profiles: Development, Analysis, Content. He is directive — he reads current state and tells the user where things are and what's available.
description_normalized: Ralphy — David's Ralph Wiggum approach for autonomous batch campaigns on existing projects.

name: relay-register
description_raw: Manage relay folders — typed shared-folder channels between agent harnesses and apps, or between people. Use when user mentions: \"list relay folders\", \"relay status\", \"relay info\", \"register a relay\", \"create a relay folder\", \"open syncthing\", \"app relay for angeleye\", \"app relay with paperclip\", \"app relay with hermes\", \"people relay for Jan\", \"people relay for Lars\", \"external relay\", \"relay for david-lars\", \"project relay\", \"flihub relay\", \"migrate relay\", \"relay handover\", \"register relay from handover\", \"set up sync folder\", \"relay for beauty-and-joy\", \"relay for supportsignal\", \"what would a relay folder look like\".
description_normalized: Manage relay folders — typed shared-folder channels between agent harnesses and apps, or between people.

name: remote-machines
description_raw: Run commands, copy files, or execute workflows on David's other machines via SSH. Use when user says "run this on the mac mini", "run it on the other machine", "ssh to mac-mini", "deploy to the agent machine", "bot machine", "headless machine", "workstation machine", "send this to mac-mini-m4", "mac-mini-m2", "remote machines", "other machines", or any request involving executing something on a machine other than the current one. Also use proactively when a task would clearly benefit from running on a specific machine (e.g. a background job on the headless agent machine).
description_normalized: Run commands, copy files, or execute workflows on David's other machines via SSH.

name: session-checkpoint
description_raw: Checkpoint current task and workflow progress mid-session so it can be restored exactly. Use when user says "checkpoint", "save my progress", "checkpoint the workflow", "where am I up to", "save task state", "checkpoint before this next step", or when about to do a risky operation or context is filling up. Captures step-by-step progress through a plan or task list — NOT a session summary (use capture-context) and NOT durable knowledge (use knowledge-capture).
description_normalized: Checkpoint current task and workflow progress mid-session so it can be restored exactly.

name: debugging-and-error-recovery
description_raw: Guides systematic root-cause debugging. Use when tests fail, builds break, behavior doesn't match expectations, or you encounter any unexpected error. Use when you need a systematic approach to finding and fixing the root cause rather than guessing.
description_normalized: Provides a structured triage process for root-cause debugging when tests fail, builds break, or runtime behavior is unexpected.

frontmatter_yaml.name: debugging-and-error-recovery
frontmatter_yaml.description: Guides systematic root-cause debugging. Use when tests fail, builds break, behavior doesn't match expectations, or you encounter any unexpected error. Use when you need a systematic approach to finding and fixing the root cause rather than guessing.

name: frontend-ui-engineering
description_raw: Builds production-quality UIs. Use when building or modifying user-facing interfaces. Use when creating components, implementing layouts, managing state, or when the output needs to look and feel production-quality rather than AI-generated.
description_normalized: Builds accessible, performant, design-system-adherent UIs that look professionally crafted rather than AI-generated.

frontmatter_yaml.name: frontend-ui-engineering
frontmatter_yaml.description: Builds production-quality UIs. Use when building or modifying user-facing interfaces. Use when creating components, implementing layouts, managing state, or when the output needs to look and feel production-quality rather than AI-generated.

name: git-workflow-and-versioning
description_raw: Structures git workflow practices. Use when making any code change. Use when committing, branching, resolving conflicts, or when you need to organize work across multiple parallel streams.
description_normalized: Enforces disciplined git workflow (trunk-based, atomic commits, safe branching) as the safety net for AI-accelerated development.

frontmatter_yaml.name: git-workflow-and-versioning
frontmatter_yaml.description: Structures git workflow practices. Use when making any code change. Use when committing, branching, resolving conflicts, or when you need to organize work across multiple parallel streams.

name: incremental-implementation
description_raw: Delivers changes incrementally. Use when implementing any feature or change that touches more than one file. Use when you're about to write a large amount of code at once, or when a task feels too big to land in one step.
description_normalized: Implements features in thin vertical slices — build one piece, test it, verify it, then expand — never writing large batches before testing.

frontmatter_yaml.name: incremental-implementation
frontmatter_yaml.description: Delivers changes incrementally. Use when implementing any feature or change that touches more than one file. Use when you're about to write a large amount of code at once, or when a task feels too big to land in one step.

name: performance-optimization
description_raw: Optimizes application performance. Use when performance requirements exist, when you suspect performance regressions, or when Core Web Vitals or load times need improvement. Use when profiling reveals bottlenecks that need fixing.
description_normalized: Measures then fixes performance bottlenecks — Core Web Vitals, load times, SLAs — never optimizing before profiling proves a problem exists.

frontmatter_yaml.name: performance-optimization
frontmatter_yaml.description: Optimizes application performance. Use when performance requirements exist, when you suspect performance regressions, or when Core Web Vitals or load times need improvement. Use when profiling reveals bottlenecks that need fixing.

name: security-and-hardening
description_raw: Hardens code against vulnerabilities. Use when handling user input, authentication, data storage, or external integrations. Use when building any feature that accepts untrusted data, manages user sessions, or interacts with third-party services.
description_normalized: Applies security-first practices to any feature touching user input, authentication, data storage, or external integrations — treating all external input as hostile.

frontmatter_yaml.name: security-and-hardening
frontmatter_yaml.description: Hardens code against vulnerabilities. Use when handling user input, authentication, data storage, or external integrations. Use when building any feature that accepts untrusted data, manages user sessions, or interacts with third-party services.

name: source-driven-development
description_raw: Grounds every implementation decision in official documentation. Use when you want authoritative, source-cited code free from outdated patterns. Use when building with any framework or library where correctness matters.
description_normalized: Grounds every framework-specific implementation decision in official documentation with source citations, preventing stale training-data patterns.

frontmatter_yaml.name: source-driven-development
frontmatter_yaml.description: Grounds every implementation decision in official documentation. Use when you want authoritative, source-cited code free from outdated patterns. Use when building with any framework or library where correctness matters.

name: build
description_raw: Implement the next task incrementally — build, test, verify, commit
description_normalized: Orchestration entry point that invokes incremental-implementation and test-driven-development together to build and verify the next planned task.

frontmatter_yaml.description: Implement the next task incrementally — build, test, verify, commit

name: browser-testing-with-devtools
description_raw: Tests in real browsers via Chrome DevTools MCP. Use when building or debugging anything that runs in a browser. Use when you need to inspect the DOM, capture console errors, analyze network requests, profile performance, or verify visual output with real runtime data. Requires the chrome-devtools MCP server to be configured.
description_normalized: Uses Chrome DevTools MCP to give agents live browser inspection — DOM, console, network, and performance — instead of static code analysis.

frontmatter_yaml.name: browser-testing-with-devtools
frontmatter_yaml.description: Tests in real browsers via Chrome DevTools MCP. Use when building or debugging anything that runs in a browser. Use when you need to inspect the DOM, capture console errors, analyze network requests, profile performance, or verify visual output with real runtime data. Requires the chrome-devtools MCP server to be configured.

name: code-simplification
description_raw: Simplifies code for clarity. Use when refactoring code for clarity without changing behavior. Use when code works but is harder to read, maintain, or extend than it should be. Use when reviewing code that has accumulated unnecessary complexity.
description_normalized: Reduces code complexity while preserving exact behavior, targeting readability over line-count reduction.

frontmatter_yaml.name: code-simplification
frontmatter_yaml.description: Simplifies code for clarity. Use when refactoring code for clarity without changing behavior. Use when code works but is harder to read, maintain, or extend than it should be. Use when reviewing code that has accumulated unnecessary complexity.

name: deprecation-and-migration
description_raw: Manages deprecation and migration. Use when removing old systems, APIs, or features. Use when migrating users from one implementation to another. Use when deciding whether to maintain or sunset existing code.
description_normalized: Manages safe removal of old systems and migration of users from deprecated to new implementations.

frontmatter_yaml.name: deprecation-and-migration
frontmatter_yaml.description: Manages deprecation and migration. Use when removing old systems, APIs, or features. Use when migrating users from one implementation to another. Use when deciding whether to maintain or sunset existing code.

name: test-driven-development
description_raw: Drives development with tests. Use when implementing any logic, fixing any bug, or changing any behavior. Use when you need to prove that code works, when a bug report arrives, or when you're about to modify existing functionality.
description_normalized: Writes failing tests before implementation, treating tests as proof — not optional verification — for every logic change or bug fix.

frontmatter_yaml.name: test-driven-development
frontmatter_yaml.description: Drives development with tests. Use when implementing any logic, fixing any bug, or changing any behavior. Use when you need to prove that code works, when a bug report arrives, or when you're about to modify existing functionality.

name: test-engineer
description_raw: QA engineer specialized in test strategy, test writing, and coverage analysis. Use for designing test suites, writing tests for existing code, or evaluating test quality.
description_normalized: QA-engineer persona that designs test suites, writes tests for existing code, and identifies coverage gaps.

frontmatter_yaml.name: test-engineer
frontmatter_yaml.description: QA engineer specialized in test strategy, test writing, and coverage analysis. Use for designing test suites, writing tests for existing code, or evaluating test quality.

name: test
description_raw: Run TDD workflow — write failing tests, implement, verify. For bugs, use the Prove-It pattern.
description_normalized: Orchestration entry point that invokes test-driven-development for new features (write-fail-implement-verify) and the Prove-It pattern for bugs.

name: code-simplify
description_raw: Simplify code for clarity and maintainability — reduce complexity without changing behavior
description_normalized: Orchestration entry point that invokes code-simplification to reduce complexity while preserving exact behavior.

name: baku
description_raw: Sync and manage Baku app zips from claude.ai (claude-ship exports) to ~/Downloads/baku-2.0/. Use when David says 'sync baku', 'baku sync', 'baku status', 'update baku-2.0', 'new zip downloaded', 'identify baku app', or asks about downloaded project zips. Keeps baku-2.0/ current with the latest snapshot of each app, identified by component fingerprints in signatures.json.
description_normalized: Sync and manage Baku app zips from claude.ai (claude-ship exports) to ~/Downloads/baku-2.0/.

name: ci-cd-and-automation
description_raw: Automates CI/CD pipeline setup. Use when setting up or modifying build and deployment pipelines. Use when you need to automate quality gates, configure test runners in CI, or establish deployment strategies.
description_normalized: Sets up and modifies CI/CD pipelines to automate quality gates, test runners, and deployment strategies.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ci-cd-and-automation
frontmatter_yaml.description: Automates CI/CD pipeline setup. Use when setting up or modifying build and deployment pipelines. Use when you need to automate quality gates, configure test runners in CI, or establish deployment strategies.

name: doubt-driven-development
description_raw: Subjects every non-trivial decision to a fresh-context adversarial review before it stands. Use when correctness matters more than speed, when working in unfamiliar code, when stakes are high (production, security-sensitive logic, irreversible operations), or any time a confident output would be cheaper to verify now than to debug later.
description_normalized: Materializes a fresh-context adversarial reviewer biased to disprove before any non-trivial decision stands.

frontmatter_yaml.name: doubt-driven-development
frontmatter_yaml.description: Subjects every non-trivial decision to a fresh-context adversarial review before it stands. Use when correctness matters more than speed, when working in unfamiliar code, when stakes are high (production, security-sensitive logic, irreversible operations), or any time a confident output would be cheaper to verify now than to debug later.

name: security-auditor
description_raw: Security engineer focused on vulnerability detection, threat modeling, and secure coding practices. Use for security-focused code review, threat analysis, or hardening recommendations.
description_normalized: Security-engineer persona focused on exploitable vulnerabilities, threat modeling, and practical hardening recommendations.

frontmatter_yaml.name: security-auditor
frontmatter_yaml.description: Security engineer focused on vulnerability detection, threat modeling, and secure coding practices. Use for security-focused code review, threat analysis, or hardening recommendations.

name: false-twins
description_raw: Differential analysis for two systems, tools, methodologies, products, or platforms that claim to solve the same problem but may differ in mechanism, philosophy, economics, or operational shape. Eight-lens framework plus triangulation. Use when the user says 'false twins', 'differential analysis', 'compare X and Y', 'X vs Y deep dive', 'lookalike audit', 'is this really the same as', 'two systems same problem', 'should I use X or Y', 'what's the actual difference between', 'these look the same — are they?', or when evaluating two competing libraries / SaaS products / methodologies / community platforms / models / vendors that appear interchangeable on the surface.
description_normalized: Differential analysis for two systems, tools, methodologies, products, or platforms that claim to solve the same problem but may differ in mechanism, philosophy, economics, or operational shape.

name: prompt-injection-scanner
description_raw: Scan untrusted content for prompt injection attacks before it enters any pipeline. Wraps content in an isolated Opus agent for model-pinned, auditable sanitization. Use when: 'scan this for injection', 'sanitize this content', 'check this before processing', 'is this safe to process?', 'scan incoming email / OMI / document / search result', or any time external content (email, web, relay, uploaded files, client documents) is about to be processed by a downstream skill or pipeline.
description_normalized: Scan untrusted content for prompt injection attacks before it enters any pipeline.

name: truth-trail
description_raw: Knowledge integrity rules system for second-brain agents. Enforces the four pillars — Canonical, Synthesis, Citation, Provenance Chain — whenever any agent writes a synthesis document (derivative created from a source of truth). Consult truth-trail before writing any _synth.md, session summary, brief, skill, doc, or other derivative document. Trigger on phrases like "write a synthesis", "create a summary from", "derive a document", "produce a brief from", "write up from the transcript", "synthesise this", "capture knowledge from", "create a skill from", "truth trail", "truth-trail", "provenance chain", "check citation", "canonical source", "knowledge integrity", "orphaned knowledge", "unverified synthesis", "verify provenance", "enforce truth trail", "is this cited?", "does this have a canonical?".
description_normalized: Knowledge integrity rules system for second-brain agents.

name: ux-writer
description_raw: Expert UX analyst, interaction designer, and experience strategist. Takes a long, messy brainstorm — conversations, session notes, sketches described in words, rough ideas — and transforms it into a clear, structured, decision-useful UX requirements document. Produces screen inventories, user flows, interaction specifications, information architecture, and UX business rules. Use when user says: "ux writer", "write the UX spec", "document the UX", "UX requirements", "interaction spec", "write up the user flows", "document how this works from a UX perspective", "turn this into a UX spec", "what are the screens", "map the user journey", "write the experience requirements". Works on any app, domain, or system.
description_normalized: Expert UX analyst, interaction designer, and experience strategist.

name: uat
description_raw: User Acceptance Testing command for FliVideo — run UAT scenarios and report results.
description_normalized: Run user acceptance testing scenarios and report results for FliVideo.

name: code-review-and-quality
description_raw: Conducts multi-axis code review. Use before merging any change. Use when reviewing code written by yourself, another agent, or a human. Use when you need to assess code quality across multiple dimensions before it enters the main branch.
description_normalized: Conducts multi-axis code review (correctness, readability, architecture, security, performance) before any change is merged.

frontmatter_yaml.name: code-review-and-quality
frontmatter_yaml.description: Conducts multi-axis code review. Use before merging any change. Use when reviewing code written by yourself, another agent, or a human. Use when you need to assess code quality across multiple dimensions before it enters the main branch.

name: documentation-and-adrs
description_raw: Records decisions and documentation. Use when making architectural decisions, changing public APIs, shipping features, or when you need to record context that future engineers and agents will need to understand the codebase.
description_normalized: Records architectural decisions and context (why, not just what) via ADRs so future engineers and agents can understand the codebase.

frontmatter_yaml.name: documentation-and-adrs
frontmatter_yaml.description: Records decisions and documentation. Use when making architectural decisions, changing public APIs, shipping features, or when you need to record context that future engineers and agents will need to understand the codebase.

name: shipping-and-launch
description_raw: Prepares production launches. Use when preparing to deploy to production. Use when you need a pre-launch checklist, when setting up monitoring, when planning a staged rollout, or when you need a rollback strategy.
description_normalized: Runs a pre-launch checklist ensuring any production deployment is reversible, observable, and incremental with a rollback plan.

frontmatter_yaml.name: shipping-and-launch
frontmatter_yaml.description: Prepares production launches. Use when preparing to deploy to production. Use when you need a pre-launch checklist, when setting up monitoring, when planning a staged rollout, or when you need a rollback strategy.

name: code-reviewer
description_raw: Senior code reviewer that evaluates changes across five dimensions — correctness, readability, architecture, security, and performance. Use for thorough code review before merge.
description_normalized: Staff-Engineer-level persona that evaluates code across five quality dimensions and returns categorized, actionable feedback before merge.

frontmatter_yaml.name: code-reviewer
frontmatter_yaml.description: Senior code reviewer that evaluates changes across five dimensions — correctness, readability, architecture, security, and performance. Use for thorough code review before merge.

name: review
description_raw: Conduct a five-axis code review — correctness, readability, architecture, security, performance
description_normalized: Orchestration entry point that invokes code-review-and-quality to evaluate staged or recent changes across five quality axes.

frontmatter_yaml.description: Conduct a five-axis code review — correctness, readability, architecture, security, performance

name: ship
description_raw: Run the pre-launch checklist via parallel fan-out to specialist personas, then synthesize a go/no-go decision
description_normalized: Fan-out orchestrator that runs code-reviewer, security-auditor, and test-engineer in parallel then merges reports into a go/no-go shipping decision.

frontmatter_yaml.description: Run the pre-launch checklist via parallel fan-out to specialist personas, then synthesize a go/no-go decision

name: agent-orchestrator
description_raw: Design advisor for multi-agent orchestrated workflows using Claude Code teammate mode. Use when user wants to "design a multi-agent workflow", "set up agent orchestration", "spin up multiple agents", "build an agent pipeline", "orchestrate agents", "agent team workflow", "teammate mode setup", "how do I run multiple agents", "agent sequencing", "multi-agent gates".
description_normalized: Design advisor for multi-agent orchestrated workflows using Claude Code teammate mode.

name: agentic-capability
description_raw: Design advisor for choosing the right Claude Code extension mechanism before building anything. Covers the full agentic toolkit — skills, commands, subagents, hooks, and agent teams. Use when user asks "should I build a skill or a command?", "what's the best way to implement this?", "should this be a subagent?", "how should I structure these skills?", "I want to build X what should I use?", "skill vs command", "how do I add this capability", "design this agentic capability", "what mechanism should I use", "agentic capability", "what should I build".
description_normalized: Design advisor for choosing the right Claude Code extension mechanism before building anything.

name: appydave-thumbnail
description_raw: Generate AppyDave YouTube thumbnail concepts from a transcript and one or more focal points, then generate finished thumbnails via Kie.ai with hook text composed into the image from the start. Typography-first brand — Bebas Neue hook text, ghost watermarks, warm cream + dark brown palette. Use when creating thumbnails for AppyDave YouTube videos, or when the user says 'thumbnail', 'generate thumbnails', 'thumbnail concepts', or wants to visualise focal points from a transcript as YouTube images.
description_normalized: Generate AppyDave YouTube thumbnail concepts from a transcript and one or more focal points, then generate finished thumbnails via Kie.ai with hook text composed into the image from the start.

name: architectural-review
description_raw: Structural and design quality review of system architecture. Different from code review — this asks "is this system well-structured for growth?" not "is this code correct?". Use when user says "architectural review", "system design review", "is this ready to scale", "structural review", "before adding a major feature", "God class", "too many responsibilities", "design review", "is this maintainable", "refactor planning", "before building on top", "architecture audit", "is this well designed", "responsibility review". Does NOT replace code-quality-audit or test-quality-audit — it adds a third lens on top of them.
description_normalized: Structural and design quality review of system architecture.

name: capture-context
description_raw: Capture and summarize session context for continuity across Claude sessions. Use when user says "capture context", "save session context", "summarize this session", "create a context handover", "what should I tell the next session", "session summary", "capture what we've done", or "I'm starting a new session". Produces a compact, structured summary of the current session that can be pasted into a new Claude session to restore working context instantly. Includes active files, decisions with rationale, what was ruled out (negative knowledge), and exact next steps.
description_normalized: Capture and summarize session context for continuity across Claude sessions.

name: clipboard-intake
description_raw: Bidirectional clipboard bridge — capture clipboard content to filesystem (Mode A) or package files onto clipboard for external tools (Mode B). Use when: 'paste from clipboard', 'capture clipboard', 'save clipboard to file', 'save what I copied', 'ingest from clipboard', 'take what's on my clipboard', 'package files for clipboard', 'send these files to clipboard', 'prepare files for ChatGPT', 'prepare files for Claude Design', 'bundle files for upload', 'put these files on clipboard', 'copy files to clipboard for pasting'.
description_normalized: Bidirectional clipboard bridge — capture clipboard content to filesystem (Mode A) or package files onto clipboard for external tools (Mode B).

name: dark-factory-catalog
description_raw: Catalog and assess skills, agents, workflows, commands, templates, and hooks across the 13 dark-factory cluster repos (BMAD-METHOD, gstack, Superpowers, Agent Skills Osmani, Compound Engineering, Compound Knowledge, Everything Claude Code, Ruflo, gbrain, Spec Kit, GSD, POEM, AppyDave Plugins). Distill ~1000+ raw artifacts down to ~50 unified skills under David's system. Use when user says "dark factory catalog", "catalog the repos", "recon BMAD" or "recon any specific repo", "discover artifacts", "discover skills across repos", "tag artifact stages", "evaluate compound engineering", "distill testing skills", "distill the catalog", "catalog self-improvement", "factory agent assessor", "assess skills across repos", "build the catalog", or any operation on the dark-factory cluster artifact corpus. Six capabilities (recon / discover / tag / evaluate / distill / self-improvement) — each invokable independently. Schema is NOT defined upfront — schema discovery is a first-class capability.
description_normalized: Catalog and assess skills, agents, workflows, commands, templates, and hooks across the 13 dark-factory cluster repos (BMAD-METHOD, gstack, Superpowers, Agent Skills Osmani, Compound Engineering, Compound Knowledge, Everything Claude Code, Ruflo, gbrain, Spec Kit, GSD, POEM, AppyDave Plugins).

name: doc-review
description_raw: Orchestrate parallel documentation review across 6 quality dimensions. Launches doc-review:coherence, doc-review:gaps, doc-review:completeness, doc-review:topology, doc-review:prose, and doc-review:crossref as background agents, then triages and synthesizes their findings. Use when user mentions: 'review docs', 'doc review', 'review this brain', 'documentation quality', 'full doc audit', 'review all dimensions', 'run doc review'.
description_normalized: Orchestrate parallel documentation review across 6 quality dimensions.

name: doc-review-coherence
description_raw: Review documentation for coherence — find contradictions between documents, tonal shifts suggesting different authors or time periods, claims that may have drifted from truth, and implicit assumptions that conflict. Part of the doc-review dimension suite (CH dimension). Use when user mentions: 'check coherence', 'find contradictions', 'doc contradictions', 'content drift', 'tonal consistency', 'do docs agree'.
description_normalized: Review documentation for coherence — find contradictions between documents, tonal shifts suggesting different authors or time periods, claims that may have drifted from truth, and implicit assumptions that conflict.

name: doc-review-completeness
description_raw: Review documentation for completeness — check whether docs deliver what they promise, map stated intent to actual content, and flag scope gaps and scope creep. Part of the doc-review dimension suite (CA dimension). Use when user mentions: 'check completeness', 'does it deliver', 'scope check', 'intent vs reality', 'does it cover everything', 'scope gaps', 'scope creep'.
description_normalized: Review documentation for completeness — check whether docs deliver what they promise, map stated intent to actual content, and flag scope gaps and scope creep.

name: doc-review-crossref
description_raw: Review documentation cross-reference integrity — verify provenance chains, check that examples match reality, validate index-to-file sync, and confirm version references are current. Part of the doc-review dimension suite (XR dimension). Use when user mentions: 'check cross-references', 'provenance chain', 'examples accurate', 'index sync', 'references current', 'version check', 'source attribution'.
description_normalized: Review documentation cross-reference integrity — verify provenance chains, check that examples match reality, validate index-to-file sync, and confirm version references are current.

name: easy-git
description_raw: Safe, guided Git workflow for non-experts working across multiple machines. Use when the user wants to pull, push, sync, resolve conflicts, or check Git status without knowing Git commands. Triggers on phrases like "sync my repo", "push my changes", "I have merge conflicts", "pull latest", "git is broken", "rebase help", "can't push", "diverged branches", or any request to get a Git repo into a clean, synced state.
description_normalized: Safe, guided Git workflow for non-experts working across multiple machines.

name: enrich-metadata
description_raw: Enrich markdown documents with structured metadata (tags, topics, relationships, summaries, entities) using cheap LLM providers (Gemini free tier or Codex). Use when user mentions: 'enrich metadata', 'enrich this', 'extract metadata', 'extract tags', 'tag this document', 'classify this', 'extract ontology', 'run extraction', 'tag extraction'. Also triggered when brain-librarian needs tag suggestions or vocabulary growth candidates.
description_normalized: Enrich markdown documents with structured metadata (tags, topics, relationships, summaries, entities) using cheap LLM providers (Gemini free tier or Codex).

name: handover-pattern
description_raw: Standard format for PO ↔ Developer communication. Use when user asks to "create a handover for the developer", "hand this to the developer", "handover to PO", "give the developer a summary", or "what should I tell the developer".
description_normalized: Standard format for PO ↔ Developer communication.

name: krisp-fetch
description_raw: Fetch meeting recordings and transcripts from the Krisp AI Meeting Assistant API. Use when user says "get krisp transcripts", "fetch krisp meetings", "what did krisp record", "krisp recordings", "pull krisp data", "show krisp meetings", "krisp transcript for today", "save krisp recordings", "list krisp meetings", "mobile recording krisp", or any request to retrieve voice recordings or transcripts captured by the Krisp mobile app or desktop app.
description_normalized: Fetch meeting recordings and transcripts from the Krisp AI Meeting Assistant API.

name: lexi
description_raw: Portable brain librarian — curate, onboard, maintain, and analyse second-brain knowledge collections (single brain or multi-brain collections). Use when setting up a new brain folder, onboarding a collection of brains, running a full health audit, adding frontmatter, populating INDEX.md, auditing a brain's compliance, syncing file counts, fixing tags, finding duplicate documents, identifying files that need splitting, finding isolated brains, finding orphan/unlinked files, auditing negative-knowledge coverage, adding aliases or tags to a brain, building the machine-readable brains-index.json, or enriching raw intake files. She introduces herself as Lexi. Trigger on phrases like "run lexi", "ask lexi", "set up a new brain", "onboard this brain", "onboard this collection", "initialize this folder", "run audit", "full audit", "audit the brain", "health check the brain", "fix the index", "clean up the brain", "check frontmatter", "add frontmatter", "sync file counts", "fix tags", "tag compliance", "what's missing in the brain", "sweep all brains", "audit the collection", "find duplicates", "search similar", "duplicate check", "find similar documents", "what's duplicated", "find documents to split", "check SRP", "single responsibility", "what needs splitting", "files that cover too much", "split candidates", "find isolated brains", "check isolation", "find orphan files", "find unlinked files", "check orphans", "check happy path", "find brains without anti-patterns", "negative knowledge audit", "add alias", "add tag", "build brains index", "rebuild brain index", "regenerate brains-index", "add brain alias", "add brain tag", "find brain by alias", "enrich raw files", "add frontmatter to intake", "health check raw intake", "normalise transcripts", "enrich transcripts".
description_normalized: Portable brain librarian — curate, onboard, maintain, and analyse second-brain knowledge collections (single brain or multi-brain collections).

name: near-compaction
description_raw: Orchestrate all context-saving actions when Claude's context window is filling up. Use when user says "near compaction", "running out of context", "context is filling up", "save everything before we compact", "pre-compaction save", "almost at limit", "context nearly full", or when you notice context warnings. Runs a triage checklist and calls leaf skills in the right order — session-checkpoint, capture-context, knowledge-capture — so nothing important is lost before compaction fires.
description_normalized: Orchestrate all context-saving actions when Claude's context window is filling up.

name: omi
description_raw: OMI pipeline orchestrator — syncs new transcripts from the OMI API then extracts and classifies them with Gemini. Use when user says: "sync and extract OMI", "run omi pipeline", "omi sync", "process omi", "omi fetch and extract", "catch up on omi", "run omi", "update omi", "update discovery queries", "refresh random context", "update omi queries".
description_normalized: OMI pipeline orchestrator — syncs new transcripts from the OMI API then extracts and classifies them with Gemini.

name: using-agent-skills
description_raw: Discovers and invokes agent skills. Use when starting a session or when you need to discover which skill applies to the current task. This is the meta-skill that governs how all other skills are discovered and invoked.
description_normalized: Meta-skill injected at session start that governs skill discovery and invocation across the entire agent-skills collection.

frontmatter_yaml.name: using-agent-skills
frontmatter_yaml.description: Discovers and invokes agent skills. Use when starting a session or when you need to discover which skill applies to the current task. This is the meta-skill that governs how all other skills are discovered and invoked.

name: ansible-fleet
description_raw: Manage and audit the Ansible-provisioned Mac fleet across David's machines (AppyDave group) and client machines (e.g. filtcore for Lars). Use when user says "audit fleet", "audit the machines", "check machine drift", "which machines have X", "add capability to ansible", "add this to the fleet", "remove from fleet", "deprecate capability", "ansible playbook", "fleet config", "group_vars", "host_vars", "fleet alignment", "machine out of sync", "what's on all my machines", "compare machines", "fleet inventory", "provision machine". The skill has three capabilities: (1) Fleet Audit — produce a drift/alignment report across all machines, (2) Capability Lifecycle — add/promote/deprecate a capability with Ansible-compatible classification, (3) Config Variation Management — explain where a setting lives (all/group/host).
description_normalized: Manage and audit the Ansible-provisioned Mac fleet across David's machines (AppyDave group) and client machines (e.g.

name: apps-registry
description_raw: Read, write, and shape the canonical AppyDave app registry at ~/.config/appydave/apps.json. Use when the user says: "add app", "register app", "retire app", "archive app", "list apps", "find app", "what apps do I have", "apps by family", "apps by runtime", "matrix data", "registry stats", "apps for [machine]", "update app", "move app to family", "validate registry", or "migrate registry".
description_normalized: Read, write, and shape the canonical AppyDave app registry at ~/.config/appydave/apps.json.

name: bmad-story-lifecycle
description_raw: Launches and manages the complete BMAD story lifecycle using Claude Code multi-agent teammate orchestration. Use when user wants to "run a BMAD story", "start the story lifecycle", "run story 6.6", "bmad story run", "orchestrate a story", "run the full BMAD pipeline", "start from Amelia", "skip Bob", "bmad orchestrate". Produces infrastructure start block, orchestrator system prompt, and paste-ready agent command sequence for a given story ID.
description_normalized: Launches and manages the complete BMAD story lifecycle using Claude Code multi-agent teammate orchestration.

name: clauding-lab-events
description_raw: Create recurring Clauding Lab events on app.sola.day for the 4Seas Community. Use when user says "create Clauding Lab event", "4C event", "4Cs event", "Claude Lab event", "Claude Lab Thursday", "Clauding Lab Thursday night", "sola.day event", or "schedule Claude Lab". Handles browser automation via Playwright MCP to fill and submit the event creation form.
description_normalized: Create recurring Clauding Lab events on app.sola.day for the 4Seas Community.

name: code-quality-audit
description_raw: Deep code quality audit of production source files. Think like a senior software architect reviewing code before building a major feature on top of it. Use when user says "code quality audit", "audit the code", "review production code", "is this code ready", "grade the code", "quality check", "before building on this", "code health check", "review my implementation", "is this codebase solid". Always launch in parallel with appydave:test-quality-audit — they cover different concerns and neither substitutes for the other.
description_normalized: Deep code quality audit of production source files.

name: codebase-audit
description_raw: Three-lens codebase audit orchestrator — runs code quality, test quality, and architectural review in parallel as subagents and synthesises a combined readiness verdict. Use when user says "codebase audit", "audit the code", "run all three audits", "complete audit", "comprehensive audit", "code review audit", "is this codebase ready". For a layer-aware full system health check use appydave:system-audit instead. For single-lens audits use appydave:code-quality-audit, appydave:test-quality-audit, or appydave:architectural-review directly.
description_normalized: Three-lens codebase audit orchestrator — runs code quality, test quality, and architectural review in parallel as subagents and synthesises a combined readiness verdict.

name: conversation-triage
description_raw: Mid-conversation audit that surfaces open questions, loose threads, parked topics, decisions made, and unstated assumptions — right now, in the current session. Use when user says "what are the open items", "what did we decide", "what's unresolved", "loose ends", "what did we park", "what are we tracking", "what questions are still open", "triage the conversation", "what have we not finished", "where are we up to", "what did I ask that we never answered".
description_normalized: Mid-conversation audit that surfaces open questions, loose threads, parked topics, decisions made, and unstated assumptions — right now, in the current session.

name: skill-miner
description_raw: Mines David's past Claude Code session transcripts (JSONL files in ~/.claude/projects/) for recurring patterns and promotes them into skill candidates that would improve his workflow. Use when David says "skill-miner", "mine my skills", "skill mining", "any skill candidates", "what should be a skill", "find skill patterns", "log this for skill-miner", "any new skills emerging", "skill miner scan". Two modes — scan (retrospective batch over recent JSONLs; default 7-day window) and log (hot-capture mid-session when David notices friction worth flagging). Patterns accumulate at ~/.claude/skill-miner/candidates.md, decay after 60+ days unseen, and promote to skill-stub drafts in promoted/ at 3+ instances within 90 days. Scoring is friction × frequency × cost, not raw repetition. Retrospective counterpart to AngelEye's live ingestion.
description_normalized: Mines David's past Claude Code session transcripts (JSONL files in ~/.claude/projects/) for recurring patterns and promotes them into skill candidates that would improve his workflow.

name: ce-compound-refresh
description_raw: Refresh stale learning and pattern docs under docs/solutions/ by reviewing them against the current codebase, then updating, consolidating, or deleting drifted ones. Use when the user asks to "refresh my learnings", "audit docs/solutions/", "clean up stale learnings", or "consolidate overlapping docs", or when ce-compound flags an older doc as superseded.
description_normalized: Maintains docs/solutions/ by reviewing stale learning docs against the current codebase and updating, consolidating, or removing drifted entries.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-compound-refresh
frontmatter_yaml.description: Refresh stale learning and pattern docs under docs/solutions/

name: ce-compound
description_raw: Document a recently solved problem to compound your team's knowledge
description_normalized: Orchestrates parallel subagents to document a recently solved problem into docs/solutions/ using a dual-track bug/knowledge schema with overlap detection.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-compound
frontmatter_yaml.description: Document a recently solved problem to compound your team's knowledge

name: ce-optimize
description_raw: Run metric-driven iterative optimization loops -- define a measurable goal, run parallel experiments, measure each against hard gates or LLM-as-judge scores, keep improvements, and converge on the best solution. Use when optimizing clustering quality, search relevance, build performance, prompt quality, or any measurable outcome that benefits from systematic experimentation.
description_normalized: Runs metric-driven iterative optimization loops with parallel experiments measured against hard gates or LLM-as-judge scores, converging toward the best solution.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-optimize
frontmatter_yaml.description: Run metric-driven iterative optimization loops.
frontmatter_yaml.argument-hint: [path to optimization spec YAML, or describe the optimization goal]

name: kw:compound
description_raw: Extract and save learnings from a completed knowledge work session. Saves to docs/knowledge/ so future plans automatically find them.
description_normalized: Scans session for 1-3 compoundable learnings (insight/playbook/correction/pattern), requires user approval, checks for duplicates and stale entries via stale-knowledge-checker agent, then writes YAML-frontmatter markdown to docs/knowledge/.

frontmatter_yaml.name: kw:compound
frontmatter_yaml.description: Extract and save learnings from a completed knowledge work session. Saves to docs/knowledge/ so future plans automatically find them.

name: stale-knowledge-checker
description_raw: After a new learning is extracted, search docs/knowledge/ for entries that the new learning contradicts or supersedes. Returns candidates for update or removal. Prevents knowledge base from accumulating contradictions.
description_normalized: Runs after kw:compound extracts a new learning; scans docs/knowledge/ for contradicted, superseded, or complementary entries and returns update/remove/keep recommendations without writing any files.

frontmatter_yaml.name: stale-knowledge-checker
frontmatter_yaml.description: After a new learning is extracted, search docs/knowledge/ for entries that the new learning contradicts or supersedes. Returns candidates for update or removal. Prevents knowledge base from accumulating contradictions.

name: agent-eval
description_raw: Head-to-head comparison of coding agents (Claude Code, Aider, Codex, etc.) on custom tasks with pass rate, cost, time, and consistency metrics
description_normalized: Head-to-head comparison of coding agents (Claude Code, Aider, Codex, etc.) on custom tasks with pass rate, cost, time, and consistency metrics

frontmatter_yaml.name: agent-eval
frontmatter_yaml.description: Head-to-head comparison of coding agents (Claude Code, Aider, Codex, etc.) on custom tasks with pass rate, cost, time, and consistency metrics
frontmatter_yaml.tools: Read, Write, Edit, Bash, Grep, Glob

name: agent-payment-x402
description_raw: Add x402 payment execution to AI agents with per-task budgets, spending controls, and non-custodial wallets. Supports Base through agentwallet-sdk and X Layer through OKX Payments / OKX Agent Payments Protocol.
description_normalized: Add x402 payment execution to AI agents with per-task budgets, spending controls, and non-custodial wallets.

frontmatter_yaml.name: agent-payment-x402
frontmatter_yaml.description: Add x402 payment execution to AI agents with per-task budgets, spending controls, and non-custodial wallets. Supports Base through agentwallet-sdk and X Layer through OKX Payments / OKX Agent Payments Protocol.
